mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the predictive slope flips positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid convergence of the federal funds rate toward the CPIAUCSL growth rate signals impending monetary accommodation, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL monthly change falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: The real rate differential fails to predict 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or positive returns do not materialize when the differential narrows below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which systematically increases corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward test, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales indicates efficient supply chain utilization without demand destruction, which supports corporate margin expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 0.5% MoM and RSAFS change between -0.5% and +1.0%
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the mechanism reverses direction when HOUST declines by > 5% month-over-month.
